back_translation: Paul, an apostle—one who was not appointed by humans nor through a human, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised Him up from death—
and all the brothers and sisters who are with me, to: the churches in the region of Galatia
May grace and peace from God our Father, and from Jesus Christ our Lord, abide with all of you
who gave Himself up for our sins, in order to deliver us out from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father
May the glory be to Him forever and ever. Amen
I am amazed that you are turning away so quickly like this from the One who called you by the grace of Christ, to a different gospel
which in fact is not another gospel; it is only that there are some who are stirring up trouble for you, and want to distort the gospel of Christ
But even if we ourselves, or an angel from heaven, were to come and proclaim a gospel to you that differs from the gospel which we proclaimed to you, let that one be cursed
As we have said before, now I say again, if anyone proclaims to you a gospel that differs from what you received, let that one be cursed
For now, am I seeking approval from humans or from God? Or am I aiming to please humans? If I were still pleasing humans, I would not be a servant of Christ
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel which I proclaimed does not come from humans
For I did not receive that gospel from any human, and no one taught me, but I received it through revelation from Jesus Christ
You have heard of my former way of life when I still observed Judaism—that I persecuted the church of God exceedingly, and tried to destroy that church
I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries in my nation, because I had a heart strictly devoted to the traditions of my ancestors exceedingly
But when God, who set me apart from being in my mother's womb, and called me by His grace, was pleased to
reveal His Son in me, so that I might proclaim that Son among the Gentiles, I did not consult with flesh and blood immediately
nor did I go up to the city of Jerusalem to meet with the apostles who were before me, but I went out to the region of Arabia, and returned to the city of Damascus again
Then after three years, I went up to the city of Jerusalem, to make acquaintance with Cephas, and stayed with him fifteen days
But I did not meet any other apostle, except James, the younger brother of the Lord
Concerning what I write to you here, behold, I am not speaking falsely before the face of God
Then afterward, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia
But the churches in the region of Judea, which are in Christ, did not yet know my face/appearance
They only heard the report saying, 'The one who used to persecute us is now proclaiming the faith that he once tried to destroy'
And they offered glory to God because of me
